Hello Experts,

We are facing an issue in SAP Analytics Cloud starting from 10th February.

Our report uses a script on the table on result changed event and Batch Export to generate PDFs. Until 9th February, it was working correctly.

Now:

The table data changes correctly for each Tract during Batch Export.

However, the text boxes are not updating.

The values shown on the first page of the PDF are being repeated on all subsequent pages.

No changes were made to the model, script, or report configuration from our side.

Could you please confirm if there was any update or patch released around 10th February-2026 In SAP SAC that might have impacted Batch Export or scripting behavior?

Thanks in advance.

Sumit

entry.title: Re: One Delivery Order with multiple PO numbers generates...
entry.content:
Dear @JasClemencio,

Question 1.

Multiple customer PO numbers in one delivery do not automatically force an invoice split. The split occurs if billing header split-relevant fields are determined from values that differ per item/order. The most typical split triggers are:

  • Reference field on the billing header (FI Reference / VBRK-XBLNR) if configured to be derived from Customer PO number then different POs = different header value = billing split is required because the billing header can only hold one reference value
  • Assignment number on the billing header (FI Assignment / VBRK-ZUONR) same logic if derived from a value that differs by item/order, it can force a split
  • Different header partners (payer/bill-to) or key header conditions like payment terms / incoterms / currency / company code coming from different underlying sales documents. Split analysis will tell you exactly which header partners/fields differ

SAP Notes below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3009009

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2800221

Also sanity-check that the split isn’t self-inflicted by the billing run:

  • In Create Billing Documents app, if someone selected Create separate billing document for each item of billing due list, SAP will split even when consolidation would otherwise be possible. SAP Note 2800221 above

Question 2.

Point A. Split Analysis first?

SAP explicitly recommends running split analysis to identify the differing header partners / header fields that caused the split

SAP Notes below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3009009

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2800221

Point B. Review Copying Control for Billing Documents in CBC

In Public Cloud what you’d call VTFL on-prem corresponds to the SSCUIs for copying control:

  • ID 102762 Define Copying Control for Sales Document to Billing Document
  • ID 102763 Define Copying Control for Delivery Document to Billing Document
  • ID 102764 Define Copying Control for Billing Document to Billing Document, if relevant

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3269803

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3009009

In those steps, check how the system derives billing header fields, especially:

  • Reference number determination the Note lists options like Customer PO / Sales order / Delivery / External delivery / Current invoice
    If you currently copy Customer PO into the billing header reference then multiple POs in one delivery will naturally split

Point C. Check whether any custom data transfer routine is influencing header fields

If you have any custom logic review Badi SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER because it can change header data in a way that triggers splits. SAP points to this Badi as the supported enhancement point for influencing billing document creation / data transfer

Question 3.

Usually yes if the split is caused by how header fields are being filled especially Reference/Assignment.
The standard fix is to ensure that the fields SAP uses at billing header level are not derived from values that differ across the delivery items like multiple customer PO numbers

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3009009

Important. The billing header has only one customer PO / reference type value. So if the business expects one invoice and still wants to see all PO numbers the typical standard approach is:

  • keep one consolidated invoice and
  • show Customer PO per item or as a list in the output/layout

If the business requires the billing header to contain a concatenated list of multiple PO numbers not just item-level display that typically becomes an enhancement topic via SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER + custom field because standard header fields won’t store multiple PO numbers cleanly

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2800221

Summary:

  1. Run Split Analysis between the two invoices and note the exact field(s) that differ
  2. If it’s Reference / Assignment adjust ID 102762/102763/102764 so those fields don’t derive from Customer PO or derive consistently and re-test
  3. Confirm nobody is using separate billing doc per due list item in the billing run settings

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: MyInbox App in Work Build Zone only scrolls to currently selected item
entry.content:
The following behavior is currently persistent in our MyInbox in the Build Work Zone:

- We have selected an item from the list which is either a) not at the top of the list or b) will not be at the top of the list after a refresh.

After pressing the refresh button, the "All tasks" bar doesn't slide to the top to showcase the latest document that has come in or may be hidden there - this is indicated in the screenshot below. So there may always be any items at the top of the list which we don't see.

We are calling this in our code by 

this.getInboxAPI()?.updateTaskList();

This is internally somehow delegated to 

 

this.getOwnerComponent().getEventBus().publish("com.sap.spa.inbox", "refreshListInternal")

The question now is, do we have any further control over this?

This session will help you understand the relevant components of an adequate adoption program to maximize your cloud investment and ensure sustained business value. entry.title: Issue with Credit Management Not Generated Automatically for Certain Business Partners in SAP S/4HAN
entry.content:
The topic is Credit Management.

For some Business Partners, when I create a sales order through VA01, the system does not generate the credit commitment. However, if I later run the credit rebuild program UKM_COMMITMENT_REBUILD, the order is correctly picked up and included in the credit management calculation.

I performed a test in the test environment, for the same BP, and there it works automatically.

For other BPs, the calculation works automatically as expected.
The sales orders are always created using the same document type and the same transaction.

I also carried out a comparison between BPs where the calculation works automatically and the one where it does not, and I did not find any differences. The configuration is the same.

Has anyone ever encountered this type of issue?

Thank you very much.

entry.title: Re: Saved SAP Systems in VS Code using SAP Fiori tools
entry.content: Based on the following thread, https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/issue-vscode-connect-to-abap-on-btp/qaq-p/14327336 the issue is related to the ABAP Cloud host. Please ensure you specify the host as the ABAP endpoint defined in your ABAP Cloud service instance details, found in your SAP BTP cockpit under Instances and Subscriptions.  If it contains `ui` in the endpoint, then you are pointing to the wrong URL.

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 - Component-Based Architecture: Shell, Feature and Modules
entry.content:
Hi, thank you for the comment! 🙂

For us, it was important that the modules are as independent and reusable as possible.

We always consider which minimal information a module needs to perform its task autonomously and design it so that it does not need to know its feature.

In most cases this is just a single parameter (e.g., an object GUID) like in our change history module. After that, the module retrieves and processes its data independently. Because of this, the same module can be reused across multiple features (e.g. inside a monorepo) and could also be integrated into other applications if required.

As long as a module only requires parameters and has no knowledge of the feature, it remains reusable for us.

entry.title: Agentic case resolution – Accelerating AI In Support
entry.content:
We are proud to announce the go-live of the agentic case resolution (ACR) minimum viable product (MVP).  A major milestone in our journey to transform how we handle support cases and deliver value to our customers, this launch introduces that streamline repetitive tasks, reduce manual effort, and provide intelligent guidance and automation throughout the case resolution process.  

What’s New? 

The new agentic platform enables the integration of AI task agents that produce recommendations for new cases, tailored for both customers and engineers. It provides a foundation that supports the incremental expansion of additional agents into the workflow, allowing a modular and flexible approach as the process evolves over time.

For our support engineers this means:

  • Ready‑to‑review, AI‑generated response drafts for new incoming cases. Engineers can update, refine, and append these drafts with their own insights before sending, maintaining high quality while reducing repetitive work. 
  • Detecting and highlighting potential duplicate cases, helping ensure faster triage and reducing unnecessary customer wait time.
  • Component change suggestions that support engineers can accurately route cases and accelerate resolution time. 

How does it work?  

When a new case is created, the  agentic case resolution triggers a set of AI agents to analyze the case’s initial details to begin troubleshooting. For eligible cases with very high confidence, it can automatically provide a recommended response to customers for newly created Priority 3 (medium) and Priority 4 (low) cases, without direct engineer involvement. Should an engineer be required, it summarizes the initial case description and suggests a recommended response for review, helping customer receive faster, more consistent responses with reduced effort.  

What’s In It for Me? 

Customers & Partners  

  • Consistent, high-quality AI support

  • Enhanced overall customer experience by leveraging AI solutions to drive efficiency and reduce customer effort 
  • Resolution times significantly reduced through more efficient case workflows for selective Priority 3 (medium) and Priority 4 (low) cases 

Our People 

  • Reduces engineer effort 
  • Improved efficiency in case handling workflows 
  • Streamlines tools and processes into one UI to consume AI services more easily 
  • Reduces repetitive tasks 
  • Reduces task switching 

What’s coming next?   
This technology enables us to continuously add new AI task agents to the workflow in a scalable manner to expand functionality and scope.   In upcoming releases, you’ll see added capabilities like ongoing case response assistance, conversational experience, missing-information and outage detection agents, triage agent including automatic priority change request detector, and more. 

The Goal? Effortless, transparent and transformative. 

Want to know more? 

  • Experience the next evolution of SAP support with Agentic Case Resolution 
  • Boost your knowledge of SAP Support with Knowledge-Driven AI 
  • Discover the vision - visit the ACR One Slider

entry.content:
Requirement to mass changes to the output item of billing documents.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to mass changes the output item of billing docume...
entry.content:
Mass Change of Output Items in Billing Documents - App ID: F7491

With this app, you can perform mass changes on output items in billing documents that have already been posted to financial accounting. The app helps you delete erroneously determined output items, determine new output items to replace them, and send the resulting output to the relevant output channels.

You can use this app to do the following:

  • Immediately start jobs or schedule jobs that perform one or more of the following mass changes on output items of billing documents already posted to financial accounting:

    • Delete existing output items that the system has determined and whose status indicates that they are currently in preparation or still to be output

    • Retrigger the determination of output items (typically after you have made corrections in the Output Parameter Determination app to ensure that the incorrect parameters have been resolved)

    • Trigger the sending of output to the relevant output channels

  • Use selection criteria (for example, a range of billing document numbers, a specific billing date or billing date range, or a certain sold-to party) to restrict your mass changes to the relevant billing documents only

  • Copy and modify existing jobs

  • Use the job log to check whether the mass changes were successful and analyze issues.

entry.title: Tracking Historical Changes in Public Organization Dimension for SAC Planning
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

In our SAC HR Workforce planning project, I would like to implement version control for changes in the public organization dimension at the hierarchy level. Specifically, when I create the plan for a given year based on the organizational structure at that time, and the following year the organization changes and I create a new plan accordingly, I want to be able to view the previous year's plan in the story according to the organizational structure that was valid then—not the new one. I maintain the organizational structure as a custom public dimension of type 'organization'.

Any advice or best practices on how to achieve this kind of historical tracking for the public organization dimension would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

@Sac

entry.title: Dropdown is not showing for Dynamic Parameter, while launching crystal report using VB.Net.
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, I am new here. If I am posting in a wrong group or tags please let me know. I'll modify accordingly.

I have created a report by using "SAP Crystal Reports 2025" here is the report layout 

I have 2 parameter which I am using in the report 1. Provide Currency(Dynamic -> Currency.currency) 2. Static Parameter(Static). 

 

When I preview it in the designer, I get dropdown to select from both the parameter. 1. From DB 2. From the static parameters. I am using 64 bit ODBC file DSN to connect. 

Now I am trying to impletement the same thing using VB.net where once user clicks on a button it should connect to the DB using ODBC(I am using Progress 4gl db and 64 bit progress 4gl driver for 12.2). Below is a simple VB.net form to launch a report while clicking on a button.

Imports System.Data
Imports System.Data.Odbc
Imports CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine
Imports CrystalDecisions.Shared
Imports CrystalDecisions.Windows.Forms

Public Class Form1

    Private Sub Button1_Click(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles Button1.Click
        Dim reportPath = "Reprot Absolute Path"
        Dim dsnName = "File DSNname With Extension"

        Dim report As New ReportDocument()
        report.Load(reportPath)
        MessageBox.Show("Report loaded successfully. Setting database logon info...")

        ' DEBUG: Display all parameters in the report


        For Each table As CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.Table In report.Database.Tables
            Dim tableLogonInfo As TableLogOnInfo = table.LogOnInfo
            tableLogonInfo.ConnectionInfo.ServerName = dsnName
            tableLogonInfo.ConnectionInfo.DatabaseName = "DB Name"
            tableLogonInfo.ConnectionInfo.UserID = "UserId"
            tableLogonInfo.ConnectionInfo.Password = "Password"
            table.ApplyLogOnInfo(tableLogonInfo)
        Next

        For Each section As Section In report.ReportDefinition.Sections
            For Each obj As ReportObject In section.ReportObjects
                If TypeOf obj Is SubreportObject Then
                    Dim subreport = CType(obj, SubreportObject)
                    Dim subReportDoc = subreport.OpenSubreport(subreport.SubreportName)
                    For Each table As CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.Table In subReportDoc.Database.Tables
                        Dim tableLogonInfo As TableLogOnInfo = table.LogOnInfo
                        tableLogonInfo.ConnectionInfo.ServerName = dsnName
                        tableLogonInfo.ConnectionInfo.DatabaseName = "DB Name"
                        tableLogonInfo.ConnectionInfo.UserID = "UserID"
                        tableLogonInfo.ConnectionInfo.Password = "Password"
                        table.ApplyLogOnInfo(tableLogonInfo)
                    Next
                End If
            Next
        Next

        MessageBox.Show("Database logon info set successfully. Verifying database...")
        report.VerifyDatabase()
        report.Refresh()
        MessageBox.Show("Database verified and report refreshed successfully. Displaying report...")

        Dim viewer As New CrystalReportViewer()
        viewer.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        viewer.ReportSource = report

        Dim reportForm As New Form()
        reportForm.Text = "Report"
        reportForm.WindowState = FormWindowState.Maximized
        reportForm.Controls.Add(viewer)
        reportForm.ShowDialog(Me)
        MessageBox.Show("Report loaded successfully.")
    End Sub
End Class

Once I click on the button I get the report as below. Which doesn't have the dropdown for the dynamic parameter where as the dropdown is avilable for the static parameter. I am trying to understand if I am missing something  while connecting through VB.net? 

Please fell free to ask any question or details. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Process to update vendor SDS in SAP S/4H Product Compliance (Private Cloud)
entry.content: What is the end-to-end process for updating vendor SDS in SAP Product Compliance? Please outline how the product steward is notified of changes in composition or documentation from vendor side. Is there any standard capability in S/4HANA to handle this?

entry.title: Shape the Future of SAP Concur with SAP Customer Engagement Initiative: Cycle 1 - 2026
entry.content:
It’s time for something new in travel and expense

No one connects expense, travel, and invoice on a single platform like SAP Concur.

Connect financial data and take control of spending. By automating spending processes your business can run efficiently anywhere, anytime, and under any circumstances.

 

SAP Concur is constantly evolving, and you have the opportunity to shape its future. Scroll through the list of SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects for SAP Concur and get a sneak peek at the planned new features and functions.

 

Scroll through the list of SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects for SAP Concur and get informed about the planned new features and functions.

And don’t forget to register for one of the projects until March 13, 2026

SAP Concur Solutions projects for registration:

 

  • Intelligent Audit Evolution: Accelerating Audits with Automation and AI
  • New User Onboarding Experience in SAP Concur
  • Reimagining Travel & Expense with SAP Concur-embedded virtual cards experience
  • SAP Concur Expense: Streamlining Corporate Card Administration
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) – Data Platform
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) – Recommendations
  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/4173
  • Trip Extras – Connecting suppliers to business travelers when it matters most
  • Verify: Shaping the Future of Auditing and Compliance

 

 

Visit the Customer Influence website on influence.sap.com to register.

If you are also interested in other CEI projects that are currently open for registration, see here the full list.

Thank you in advance for your participation!

 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: SAP Signavio Process Governance: Skipping a task in a running process as admin
entry.content:
Hello together,

we are currently using SAP Signavio Process Governance and have a question regarding administrative handling of tasks.

Is there any supported way for an administrator to overwrite or change the process assignment of an existing task (for example, to correct an assignment or to skip the task entirely), without having to restart the process instance or delete the task?

We would be grateful for any guidance, best practices, or references to official documentation on how this scenario can be handled.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Kind regards

entry.title: Joule A2A: Connect Code Based Agents into Joule
entry.content:
Many organizations began early by developing their own AI agents and assistants, frequently using frameworks such as LangChain and LangGraph. This post demonstrates how a custom-built AI agent can be integrated into the Joule ecosystem by using the recently introduced pro-code extensibility features together with the A2A integration.

For the demonstration, a minimal ReAct agent implemented in Python with LangGraph is used.

The A2A Protocol (Agent2Agent Protocol) is an open standard developed to enable seamless communication and collaboration between autonomous AI agents built by different vendors and on different frameworks. It defines a common interaction model that allows agents to discover each other’s capabilities, exchange structured messages and tasks, and coordinate actions securely without exposing internal state or proprietary logic.

The protocol uses standardized components such as Agent Cards for capability discovery and supports multiple transport bindings including HTTP(S) and JSON-RPC 2.0. A2A is designed with enterprise requirements in mind, supporting asynchronous task workflows, secure communication, and interoperability across diverse agent ecosystems. Find details here.

Many practitioners in the community have already experimented with Joule Studio as the low-code environment for extending Joule. Joule now also provides pro-code extensibility. This expands the addressable scenarios beyond classic skills, enabling more complex logic, deeper structures, richer response shaping, and integration with remote agents via A2A.

Overview of Joule Capabilities

Within the pro-code model, developers define capabilities. A capability groups the "skills" Joule can invoke in response to a user request.

Two concepts are central: scenarios and functions.

A scenario acts as the entry shell of a skill. It contains a name, a description, and a set of input parameters. During skill selection, Joule evaluates the scenario description of the available list of scenarios to pick one. The scenario itself then declares which function should run and which parameters will be filled by Joule.

A function represents the executable logic. It is composed of smaller actions, for example, calling an API, sending or formatting messages, or—now newly—invoking another remote agent.

From an engineering perspective, these artifacts are authored as YAML projects and then deployed to a Joule instance.

To support this workflow, two primary tools are available: the Joule Studio Code Editor extension for Visual Studio Code, and the Joule Studio CLI. The example here focuses primarily on using the CLI.

Now let's go step by step:

Setup

For this exercise, there are two prerequisites:

First, you need to have an instance of Joule base instantiated on one of your BTP Subaccounts. If you do not yet have one, I recommend checking out our great mission around Joule Studio that includes a step to set up a minimal setup of Joule. Ideally, your company already enabled Joule for some of the Cloud applications, and you can utilize that instance. In the mission, they explain how to set up your user with the "enduser" role in BTP. For this exercise, we need some additional roles ("capabilityadmin", "extensibility_developer") as depicted below:

Second, to engage in the Pro-Code extensibility, you want to have the Joule CLI installed. Here I recommend checking out the newly released Joule Developer documentation on help.sap.

To log in against your Joule application, there are some additional steps necessary. We need to collect a set of client credentials. Since Joule is based on the new Cloud Identity Services flow, this involves a few extra steps.

1. Login to your IAS tenant:

On your BTP Subaccount where you set up Joule, you trusted an IAS tenant—found under Trust Configuration. You will need to navigate to its Admin Panel by appending /admin to the tenant URL.

2. Create new Application:

In the IAS admin panel, navigate to "Application & Resources" > "Applications". In there, you see a full list of all registered applications on that IAS tenant. Now we need to create a new one by hitting "Create" (highlighted in red). In the pop-up, we give it any name, select OpenID Connect, and leave the rest of the fields as is.

3. Add Joule application as a dependency:

Once the Application is created, we need to add a Dependency to that Application. For that purpose, we select the newly created application and navigate to "Dependencies". By clicking on "Add" we will be able to add a new one. In the pop-up, we need to give it the name "CLI2Joule". This name is mandatory. Next, we select the Joule application—you will recognize your subaccount name after the "das-ias". Finally, we can select the API and hit "Save".

4. Create Client Secret:

The following step will be to navigate to "Client Authentication", still under your own created app. We want to add a new Secret by clicking "Add". Giving it an arbitrary name and ticking all the boxes will lead to its creation:

Now with that, we have all the necessary details to log in to Joule:

joule login -a https://.accounts.cloud.sap --apiurl https://.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap -c  -s  -u  -p  --store-password

Note: The Authentication URL is the Domain of your IAS tenant, so you can easily copy it from the admin panel's URL. While the API URL is the URL of Joule, so when opening up your Joule application from the BTP, you can copy that one:

1. Building the Agent with LangGraph

The first step in this journey is to build an AI agent and expose it via an A2A server. For this purpose, I am using the samples provided directly by the A2A project on GitHub.

Since LangGraph is a popular framework, I am using the LangGraph sample code here. However, you could replace it with any framework you prefer. Similarly, the programming language is flexible: A2A only defines the endpoint structure and schema. You can expose A2A servers using any technology you like. Sample code is available for a variety of languages, including Node.js and Java. For my demonstration, I am using Python deployed on SAP Cloud Foundry.

My project structure, adapted for deployment on Cloud Foundry, looks like this:

app/
├── agent.py
├── agent_executor.py
├── app.py
├── manifest.yaml
├── requirements.txt
├── runtime.txt
└── test_client.py

First, let's look at the heart of our project—the agent.py:

import os
from collections.abc import AsyncIterable
from typing import Any, Literal
import httpx
from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, ToolMessage
from langchain_core.tools import tool
from genaihub.proxy.langchain.openai import ChatOpenAI
from genaihub.proxy.core.proxyclients import getproxy_client
from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
from langgraph.prebuilt import createreactagent
from pydantic import BaseModel


proxyclient = getproxy_client('gen-ai-hub')
memory = MemorySaver()


@tool
def getexchangerate(
    currency_from: str = 'USD',
    currency_to: str = 'EUR',
    currency_date: str = 'latest',
):
    """Use this to get current exchange rate.


    Args:
        currency_from: The currency to convert from (e.g., "USD").
        currency_to: The currency to convert to (e.g., "EUR").
        currency_date: The date for the exchange rate or "latest". Defaults to
            "latest".


    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the exchange rate data, or an error message if
        the request fails.
    """
    try:
        response = httpx.get(
            f'https://api.frankfurter.app/{currency_date}',
            params={'from': currencyfrom, 'to': currencyto},
        )
        response.raiseforstatus()


        data = response.json()
        if 'rates' not in data:
            return {'error': 'Invalid API response format.'}
        return data
    except httpx.HTTPError as e:
        return {'error': f'API request failed: {e}'}
    except ValueError:
        return {'error': 'Invalid JSON response from API.'}


class ResponseFormat(BaseModel):
    """Respond to the user in this format."""
    status: Literal['inputrequired', 'completed', 'error'] = 'inputrequired'
    message: str


class CurrencyAgent:
    """CurrencyAgent - a specialized assistant for currency conversions."""


    SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION = (
        'You are a specialized assistant for currency conversions. '
        "Your sole purpose is to use the 'getexchangerate' tool to answer questions about currency exchange rates. "
        'If the user asks about anything other than currency conversion or exchange rates, '
        'politely state that you cannot help with that topic and can only assist with currency-related queries. '
        'Do not attempt to answer unrelated questions or use tools for other purposes.'
    )


    FORMAT_INSTRUCTION = (
        'Set response status to input_required if the user needs to provide more information to complete the request.'
        'Set response status to error if there is an error while processing the request.'
        'Set response status to completed if the request is complete.'
    )


    def init(self):
        self.model = ChatOpenAI(
            proxymodelname='gpt-4o-mini', 
            proxyclient=proxyclient,
            temperature=0            
        )
        self.tools = [getexchangerate]


        self.graph = createreactagent(
            self.model,
            tools=self.tools,
            checkpointer=memory,
            prompt=self.SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION,
            responseformat=(self.FORMATINSTRUCTION, ResponseFormat),
        )


    async def stream(self, query, context_id) -> AsyncIterable[dict[str, Any]]:
        inputs = {'messages': [('user', query)]}
        config = {'configurable': {'threadid': contextid}}


        for item in self.graph.stream(inputs, config, stream_mode='values'):
            message = item['messages'][-1]
            if (
                isinstance(message, AIMessage)
                and message.tool_calls
                and len(message.tool_calls) > 0
            ):
                yield {
                    'istaskcomplete': False,
                    'requireuserinput': False,
                    'content': 'Looking up the exchange rates...',
                }
            elif isinstance(message, ToolMessage):
                yield {
                    'istaskcomplete': False,
                    'requireuserinput': False,
                    'content': 'Processing the exchange rates..',
                }


        yield self.getagentresponse(config)


    def getagentresponse(self, config):
        currentstate = self.graph.getstate(config)
        structuredresponse = currentstate.values.get('structured_response')
        if structured_response and isinstance(
            structured_response, ResponseFormat
        ):
            if structuredresponse.status == 'inputrequired':
                return {
                    'istaskcomplete': False,
                    'requireuserinput': True,
                    'content': structured_response.message,
                }
            if structured_response.status == 'error':
                return {
                    'istaskcomplete': False,
                    'requireuserinput': True,
                    'content': structured_response.message,
                }
            if structured_response.status == 'completed':
                return {
                    'istaskcomplete': True,
                    'requireuserinput': False,
                    'content': structured_response.message,
                }


        return {
            'istaskcomplete': False,
            'requireuserinput': True,
            'content': (
                'We are unable to process your request at the moment. '
                'Please try again.'
            ),
        }


    SUPPORTEDCONTENTTYPES = ['text', 'text/plain']

In this code snippet, several key elements are visible. First, the a2a-sdk is heavily utilized, providing the base class for the AgentExecutor. This serves as the main interface for exposing an agent through the A2A SDK. Its primary method is execute, which receives a task from the A2A client, including context and task details, passes it to the agent, and processes the response. The SDK also handles sending intermittent updates and requesting user input as needed.

Finally, there is the transport layer. The app.py file exposes the Agent Card at the well-known endpoint and manages incoming requests. Here, the transport protocol can be chosen, with the default set to JSON-RPC.

import httpx
import os
from a2a.server.apps import A2AStarletteApplication
from a2a.server.request_handlers import DefaultRequestHandler
from a2a.server.tasks import (
    BasePushNotificationSender,
    InMemoryPushNotificationConfigStore,
    InMemoryTaskStore,
)
from a2a.types import (
    AgentCapabilities,
    AgentCard,
    AgentSkill,
)
from agent import CurrencyAgent
from agent_executor import CurrencyAgentExecutor

Get host and port from environment variables (Cloud Foundry sets PORT)
HOST = os.getenv('HOST', '0.0.0.0')
PORT = int(os.getenv('PORT', 10000))

Create agent capabilities and card
capabilities = AgentCapabilities(streaming=True, push_notifications=True)
skill = AgentSkill(
    id='convert_currency',
    name='Currency Exchange Rates Tool',
    description='Helps with exchange values between various currencies',
    tags=['currency conversion', 'currency exchange'],
    examples=['What is exchange rate between USD and GBP?'],
)


agent_card = AgentCard(
    name='Currency Agent',
    description='Helps with exchange rates for currencies',
    url=f'http://localhost:443', # is ignored by the Joule A2A integration - instead the URL from the destination is taken
    version='1.0.0',
    defaultinputmodes=CurrencyAgent.SUPPORTEDCONTENTTYPES,
    defaultoutputmodes=CurrencyAgent.SUPPORTEDCONTENTTYPES,
    capabilities=capabilities,
    skills=[skill],
)

Create request handler and server
httpx_client = httpx.AsyncClient()
pushconfigstore = InMemoryPushNotificationConfigStore()
push_sender = BasePushNotificationSender(
    httpxclient=httpxclient,
    configstore=pushconfig_store
)


request_handler = DefaultRequestHandler(
    agent_executor=CurrencyAgentExecutor(),
    task_store=InMemoryTaskStore(),
    pushconfigstore=pushconfigstore,
    pushsender=pushsender
)


server = A2AStarletteApplication(
    agentcard=agentcard, 
    httphandler=requesthandler
)

Export the ASGI app for uvicorn
app = server.build()

Again, this is mostly boilerplate code provided by the A2A SDK. We populate the Agent Card with the specific information for our use case and ensure that requests are handled appropriately. To do this, we instantiate the DefaultRequestHandler.

Finally, let's look at the files that enable deployment. The manifest.yaml facilitates deployment by providing configuration settings and the startup command:

applications:
  - name: currency-agent
    memory: 512M
    disk_quota: 1G


    buildpacks:
      - python_buildpack


    env:
      # Add your SAP AI Core credentials here
      AICOREAUTHURL: ""
      AICORECLIENTID: ""
      AICORECLIENTSECRET: ""
      AICORERESOURCEGROUP: ""
      AICOREBASEURL: ""


    command: uvicorn app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${PORT}

I am using the python_buildpack in conjunction with a specific Python version specified in the runtime.txt. Since we are utilizing the foundation models of the generative AI hub of AI Core, we also ensure to pass on our credentials via environment variables.

runtime.txt:

python-3.13.9

This is necessary because the dependencies only support Python versions greater than 3.12, while Cloud Foundry currently uses an earlier version by default.

With this structure in place, the agent can be deployed to Cloud Foundry. Alternatively, it can be hosted in any other environment of your choice. Ensure to update the Agent Card with your specific URL.

2. Building the Joule Pro-Code Capability

For this example, I prepared a minimal directory and code structure that you can utilize:

currencyagentcapability/
├── functions/
│   └── currencyagentfunction.yaml
├── scenarios/
│   └── currencyagentscenario.yaml
└── capability.sapdas.yaml

Let's start with capability.sapdas.yaml. It describes the overall capability with some descriptions, defines the version of the design-time artifacts, and lets us configure the Destination mapping against the system alias.

schema_version:  3.27.0   # be aware of the minimum version for agents


metadata:
  namespace: joule.ext    # very important to be in the "joule.ext" namespace for capabilities
  name: currencyagentcapability
  version: 1.0.0
  display_name: Currency Agent Capability
  description: Capability containing the currency agent


system_aliases:
  CURRENCY_AGENT:
    destination: CURRENCY_AGENT # this is referencing the destination name

For our example, it is important to use version 3.27.0 onwards, since this is the minimum version for the Agent integration. In addition, we specify the namespace. Here we need to use the extension namespace joule.ext; other namespaces will fail.

At the bottom, we register a system alias and map it to a destination—both of which I call CURRENCY_AGENT. The destination will be named accordingly in the subsequent step in BTP.

Next, we introduce the scenario for our agent.

currencyagentscenario.yaml

description: The Currency Agent supports converting between different currencies.
target:
  name: currencyagentfunction
  type: function

It basically consists of the description and the target—in this case, the currencyagentfunction. Additionally, one could maintain parameters that would be collected by the Joule Orchestrator. For our particular case, for example, we could maintain a currency pair and an amount to convert. In addition, if your agent is handling multi-turn conversations, you might want to pass the conversation ID to it via the context.

Finally, the function:

currencyagentfunction.yaml

action_groups:
  - actions:
      - type: agent-request   # This action type allows the agent to trigger an agent
        systemalias: CURRENCYAGENT # The system alias is defined in the capability.sapdas.yaml file and points to the destination of the agent
        agent_type: remote # remote for code based agents
        result_variable: "apiResponse" # response of the agent - can be used in subsequent actions by referring to the variable name   
      - type: message
        message:
          type: text
          content: "<? apiResponse.body.artifacts[0].parts[0].text ?>"  # extracting textual response from the agent

In here, we define actions, and the most important one is the agent-request. It will invoke the Agent via the A2A integration. We specify the system alias, the type as remote, and which variable should hold the response.

Note: The response from the agent now needs to be "formatted" in this function.

In this super minimalistic case, I just output the result body in a static message sent to the user.

Understanding the Joule Agent Result Variable

When working with the agent-request action, the result_variable is the connection between the output of the agent and the formatting of the response to Joules Webclient. In that result variable we have the following:

Key Components of the Response

* Body: The core container for the task data. * History: A log of the conversation turns between the user and the agent. * Artifacts: The final, structured answers or generated content. * Status: The current state of the task (e.g., completed).

Example

Below is a sample JSON payload returned by a Joule agent after a currency conversion request:

{
  "headers": {},
  "body": {
    "kind": "task",
    "contextId": "98fa8d22-0e99-4efe-a1a9-fff535e7db9d",
    "history": [
      {
        "role": "user",
        "kind": "message",
        "parts": [{"kind": "text", "text": "Please convert 10 USD to euros"}],
        "messageId": "eb8b5afa-63cb-4cc5-9b24-38f85e782c95"
      },
      {
        "role": "agent",
        "kind": "message",
        "parts": [{"kind": "text", "text": "Looking up the exchange rates..."}]
      }
    ],
    "id": "f0d78896-0eab-472f-8a85-73050948c0a2",
    "artifacts": [
      {
        "name": "conversion_result",
        "parts": [
          {
            "kind": "text",
            "text": "10 USD is approximately 8.43 EUR based on the current exchange rate."
          }
        ],
        "artifactId": "b8d6c02b-1646-4676-969b-ca07f7df2af0"
      }
    ],
    "status": {
      "state": "completed",
      "timestamp": "2026-02-16T11:45:20.883269+00:00"
    }
  }
}

Extracting Specific Information

Developers can use simple dot-notation scripting to drill down into this variable and extract specific data for the UI.

Example: To grab the final conversion text from the JSON above, you would navigate the path:
apiResponse.body.artifacts[0].parts[0].text

This allows you to bypass the background "thought" process or status updates and display only the exact information the user is looking for in a clean, conversational message.

3. Destination Creation

To bridge Joule with our remote Python agent, we need to establish a connection via the SAP BTP Destination Service. This follows the standard procedure for creating an HTTP destination within your BTP Subaccount.

In the BTP Cockpit, navigate to Connectivity > Destinations and create a new destination with the following details:

Name: CURRENCY_AGENT (This must match the system alias we defined in our capability YAML). 
Type: HTTP 
URL: The base URL where your agent is hosted (e.g., your Cloud Foundry app URL). 
Proxy Type: Internet
Authentication: For this demonstration and to keep things simple, I have selected NoAuthentication.

The A2A integration handles the heavy lifting of discovery. When Joule triggers the agent request, it uses the URL provided in this destination to locate the well-known.json path (the discovery endpoint) of your agent.

From there, Joule retrieves the Agent Card, identifies the specific communication URL defined within that card (+ preferred protocol), and subsequently initiates A2A-compliant messaging to send tasks and receive responses from your agent.

4. Deploy the Joule Capability

Now that the Joule capability is built and the destination is configured to point toward our agent on Cloud Foundry, we can bring everything together. To do this, we will use the Joule CLI to deploy the capability to our instance.

Deployment Workflow

The Joule CLI handles the lifecycle of your capability through three main phases:

  1. Authentication: Use joule login to authenticate with the specific Joule instance where you want to deploy.
  2. Compilation: The source files must be compiled into a .daar file (Design-time Artifact Archive), which is the deployable format Joule requires.
  3. Deployment: The archive is uploaded and registered to the assistant.

Compile and deploy can be combined with the following command:

joule deploy -c -n ""

-c (Compile): Tells the CLI to first package your YAML files into the .daar format.
-n (Name): By specifying a bot name, you are creating a standalone assistant for testing purposes.

Sample Output:

joule deploy -c  -n "currencyagenttest"
✔ Building designtime artifact (currencyagentcapability)
✔ Trigger compilation (C:\Github\joule-code-based-agent-sample\currencyagentcapability)
✔ Compiled (C:\Github\joule-code-based-agent-sample\currencyagentcapability)


Detailed logs:
WARNINGS:
Message:             DTA did not define an optional i18n folder
Path:
Category:            I18N
Severity:            LOW


✔ Downloaded runtime artifact (joule.extcurrencyagentcapability1.0.0.daar)
✔ Building runtime artifact
  > joule.extcurrencyagentcapability1.0.0.daar added to the RTA
✔ Triggering deployment (currencyagenttest)
✔ Your digital assistant (currencyagenttest) deployed successfully

That would have deployed my currencyagenttest assistant that I can then launch.

Note: Test Assistant vs. Production Update

By default, Joule uses the sap_digital_assistant for live enterprise scenarios. (Also when opening Joule with /joule) When you are first developing, using joule deploy -n allows you to test your logic in a isolated "sandbox" assistant without affecting the standard content.

Once you are ready to move your capability into the main environment alongside SAP's standard content, you would typically use:

joule update "sapdigitalassistant" --capability-file capability.sapdas.yaml

This command pushes your capability into the existing sapdigitalassistant rather than creating a new standalone bot.

Launching the Assistant

After a successful deployment, the CLI will provide a URL to access your new assistant. When you open this link, you will see the assistant name reflected in the URL path.

joule launch "currencyagenttest"
✔ Launching: https://joule-pro-code-9r3t5r7a.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap/webclient/standalone/currencyagenttest

From here, you can begin interacting with your custom LangGraph agent directly through the Joule interface.

Conclusion

Finally, we can test our agent directly from within Joule. For this example, I am using a standalone Joule instance. With the necessary end-user role assigned to my user, I can open the Joule web client and ask Joule for a currency conversion:

As you can see, the scenario is selected, the function is executed, and the agent request action delegates the call to my LangGraph agent deployed on Cloud Foundry. The response is generated there and sent back to Joule. Find the full code on Github.

Nice! This opens up plenty of possibilities for extending Joule with custom-built agents.

I hope you found this insightful. Since this blog covered only a very minimal example, stay tuned for additional posts. There are many topics to dive deeper into, such as authentication flows toward SAP systems via principal propagation, managing the agent context across multi-turn conversations, and response formats beyond simple text.

entry.title: How to switch off E-Mail notifcation for flexible workflow?
entry.content:
Hi, I have got the problem that I cannot simply switch off the email notification for flexible workflow for sales objects. I have copied the email template for the sales order to xx_02000006_CRT_9 and the email is sent out correctly, whenever an approval is required. But I have got the problem, that I want to test the email notification in our test system. And we are using real users with real email adresses within our company. So we want to test, but we don't want to bother the colleagues with many unnecessary emails from our test system all the time.
That's why I would like to have the possibility to easily switch off the email notification. Either depending on the function group or on the  workflow object. Currently I have only the possibility to delete the user defined email template xx_02000006_CRT_9. And transport the changes to our test system. I could not find any customiziable flag, to stop sending emails. Could someone please give me a hint?

entry.title: Re: Dynamic excel upload using odata
entry.content:
Naga,

OData unfortunately does not support “dynamic entities” in the way you’re describing. this is best of knowledge if anyone else have more idea pls suggest.

SEGW metadata is static by design — the entity types, properties, and structure must be known at design time so the service can generate a fixed $metadata document. Because of this, you cannot dynamically expose 30 arbitrary DDIC tables through a single generic entity without creating the corresponding metadata.

That said, you do have a few clean‑core options to avoid building 30 separate entities:

1. Use a Single Generic Entity + Deep Structure

Create one entity like GenericTableUpload with fields such as:

  • TABLE_NAME

  • ROW_DATA (as JSON or stringified payload)

Your UI5 app can dynamically render fields based on metadata from a helper service (DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET, CDS metadata, etc.). Backend parses the JSON and writes to the correct table. This avoids 30 entities but requires a custom payload format.

2. Use RAP (ABAP RESTful) Instead of SEGW

RAP allows:

  • dynamic field control

  • late‑bound structures

  • generic handlers

Still not fully dynamic metadata, but far more flexible than SEGW.

3. Use File Upload + Backend Processing (No OData Entities per Table)

Expose a single OData service for file upload only. Backend reads the Excel, determines the target table from TVARVC, and processes it dynamically. No need to expose table structures through OData at all.

4. Use BTP App Router + CAP (Node.js/Java)

If you’re open to BTP: CAP can generate dynamic schemas at runtime and handle generic uploads more easily than ABAP OData.

entry.title: Re: Change of Logo
entry.content:
You can’t directly derive the SmartForm name from the graphic name because SmartForms do not store a reverse reference to the graphics they use. The graphic is stored in STXBITMAPS, but SmartForms only embed the graphic content inside the generated OTF/ITF nodes — there is no DDIC table that links a graphic to a SmartForm.

Again this best of my understanding, if anyone else have better idea, pls suggest or add comment.

That’s why your lookup stops at STXBITMAPS.

What you can do technically

You have two possible approaches:

 

1. Search SmartForm source (generated FM) for the graphic name

When a SmartForm uses a graphic, the generated function module contains a call like:

Code
 
/1BCDWB/SF... → CALL FUNCTION 'GRAPHIC_IMPORT' 
                EXPORTING name = '<graphic_name>'

So you can:

  • Read table STXFADM to get all SmartForm names

  • Get the generated FM name from STXFADM-FUNCNAME

  • Use RS_TOOL_ACCESS / READ REPORT to scan the FM source for the graphic name

  • Collect all SmartForms where the graphic name appears

This is the only reliable way.

 

2. Search SmartStyle (if used)

If the graphic is used inside a SmartStyle:

  • Read STXH/STXL for style objects

  • Parse the style XML for <GRAPHIC> tags

  • Map the style back to SmartForms that reference it

This is less common but possible.

 

Why STXBITMAPS alone is not enough

STXBITMAPS only stores the graphic itself. SmartForms embed the graphic at runtime but do not store a reference in any table. So there is no direct DB relationship you can query.

entry.title: Re: Custom field in IQ02/IQ3
entry.content:
Mike, SER06 is part of the equipment/serial number data model, and SAP does not provide an enhancement point to append custom fields directly to SER06. It’s a generated table and not designed for modification, so you cannot append a structure to it.

For IQ02/IQ03, there is also no BAdI or exit that allows you to add custom fields directly onto the standard screens. That’s why you’ve seen customers repurpose unused fields — because SAP doesn’t offer a clean extension point for SER06.

What you can do instead

1. Use the Equipment Customer Fields (CI_EQUI)

This is the standard and supported way to add custom fields to equipment master data. These fields automatically appear in IQ01/02/03 under the “Customer Fields” tab.

  • Append your field to CI_EQUI

  • Implement BAdI BADI_EQUI_UPDATE or user exit IEQM0003 if you need logic

  • Store your data in the equipment master, not SER06

This is the recommended approach.

 

2. If you must link to serial numbers

Create your own Z‑table with:

  • Equipment number

  • Serial number

  • Your custom field

Then use exits in equipment/serial processing to populate it. SER06 cannot be enhanced, but you can maintain a parallel table.

entry.title: Welcome to the SAP Sustainability Learning Group
entry.content:
In this space, you can connect with SAP experts and fellow learners across the entire spectrum of corporate sustainability—from ESG reporting to the "Green Ledger." The Sustainability Learning Group is where you will be the first to hear about our plans for introducing new courses, updates to existing sustainability learning journeys, new live sessions, and certification updates.

Our Focus: Learning for a Sustainable Future

The focus of this community is learning and knowledge building. It is not meant to replace existing SAP Community groups that focus on product-specific technical discussions or troubleshooting. Those communities still exist and should always be used whenever you have a technical question or need help with a bug on a specific product feature. There, you are more likely to reach a huge number of developers and consultants who can support your technical implementation.

This learning community is a dedicated space to ask questions and join discussions around SAP Sustainability learning content, such as:

  • Courses and Learning Journeys

  • Certification preparation

  • Live sessions and expert webinars

This is a safe space to ask even the most basic questions as you develop your expertise in sustainability management. We welcome questions related to the exercises and systems used in our official learning journeys. However, please note that this is not the place to seek support for troubleshooting product issues encountered on your own or your customer's live systems.

Empowering Your Sustainability Journey

Always remember: everyone in this group is a learner, just like you. Whether you are a sustainability officer or an IT consultant, don't hold back with your questions. We are here to help you get started and to support you if you run into trouble with topics in the learning content. Look out for blog posts from the experts that will help guide you through the complexities of ESG data.

Solutions Covered in this Community:

The solutions we cover in this community include the core of the SAP Sustainability Portfolio:

  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (Product and corporate carbon accounting)

  • SAP Sustainability Control Tower (Holistic ESG reporting and steering)

  • SAP Sustainability Data Exchange (Transparency across the value chain)

  • SAP Responsible Design and Production (Circular economy and plastic taxes)

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for EHS (Environment, Health, and Safety)

  • Circular Economy & ESG Integration within the SAP Digital Core

Moderated by Experts

The community is moderated by SAP experts who are instructors and learning content developers. This means you are connecting directly with the team who developed the course materials and certifications and who present the live sessions.

entry.title: Re: Quote 2.0 scripting: apply changes only to filtered r...
entry.content:
Hi @JC20 

In Quote 2.0, the "Filter Items" panel is strictly a UI/grid-level construct. Server-side scripting does not expose a supported mechanism to retrieve only the currently visible or filtered rows from the UI context.Quote.GetAllItems() will always return the full item collection.

From an architectural perspective, applying logic to a subset typically requires either re-evaluating the same filter criteria server-side (based on the relevant fields) or explicitly defining and passing the target item set from the UI layer to a custom server-side API script (for example, via captured item IDs stored in a controlled context).

This separation between the UI state and the server-side logic is intentional by design.

entry.title: New POD 2.0 Framework: POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series on SAP Digital Manufacturing new POD 2.0 framework, the new generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing worker UIs.

  1. New POD 2.0 Framework: The New Generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing Worker UIs
  2. New POD 2.0 Framework: Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types
  3. New POD 2.0 Framework: Visual WYSIWYG Designer

The next post on the series will be published as soon as this one reaches 20 Likes, so please leave a Like! 👍

🗓️ Timeline

POD 2.0 is currently in BETA with selected customers, General Availability (GA) is planned for 2605 release. BETA content is subject to change!

📢 POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities

The newly created POD plugins are now fully modular, offering significantly greater control and flexibility over the information displayed. This makes it possible to customize the look and feel of PODs using out-of-the-box WYSIWYG and low-code / no-code capabilities, without requiring any custom developments or coding skills.

You can customize fonts, colors, sizes, positioning, and even add or remove UI elements, and much more.

Take the POD top section as an example. In the legacy POD 1.0 framework, this area was delivered as a monolith 'SFC Card' POD plugin that used to look like this:

Design Time

Run Time

which had very little room for customizations.

In POD 2.0, the 'SFC Card' is built as a Horizontal Box:

Composed of individual Labels and Input fields, where 'Text' content is bound to the POD context data:

Combined with other modular plugins, such as 'SFC Quantity Progress'.

Which can then be fully and easily customized using simple WYSIWYG and low code / no code capabilities, including removing any objects:

Or adding new ones, including labels, input fields, buttons and much more. It is also possible to customize each individual object look and feel, including fonts, colors, sizes, and much more!

The old versus new approach shown above is a great example of the new approach of breaking legacy monolithic plugins into smaller, focused components. The benefits of this approach include:

  • Better performance

  • Cleaner configuration

  • Reduced customization overhead

  • Easier extension handling

You compose functionality instead of wrestling with oversized plugins. 🧩

In the example above, you can also see the new 'Rich Context Access' capability in action, as POD 2.0 exposes contextual manufacturing execution data directly to the UI layer, which can be bound to any other objects.

In the example below, you can see once again how the contextual manufacturing execution data of the order number can be easily bound to a label object:

So, instead of repeatedly calling backend services through APIs or production processes to retrieve data, many values are already available in context. This allows:

  • Leaner production processes

  • Faster interactions

  • Cleaner logic flows

It is a performance and maintainability win. 

The next post on the series will be published as soon as this one reaches 20 Likes, so please leave a Like! 👍

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: How to find all tables of business user / employee
entry.content:
Dear @Kristina06,

There is no single where-used app for a user across all objects. Public Cloud does not provide one universal screen that lists every place a user is referenced all workflow definitions, tolerances, responsibilities, etc. In practice you need a combination of standard apps like below

Option A: Best standard overview for user access = IAM Information System

This app is designed to show business users their assigned business roles/catalogs/restrictions and relationships/usage. It’s great for access/authorizations overview but it’s not a full where-used in all business tables finder

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/administrating-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/understanding-identity-management

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2450')/S36

Option B: Central place for lock/validity/role assignment = Maintain Business Users

SAP explicitly lists this as the place to maintain locking/unlocking, validity, settings and business role assignments

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1303')/S36

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/e40e710321c74f28916affa9ae984bce.html

Option C: If workflows use Responsibility Management = Manage Teams and Responsibilities

SAP supports Replace a Team Member across one or multiple teams via Manage Teams and Responsibilities this is the cleanest leaver handling if your workflows are team-based

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2412')/S36

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/73c04858392e9244e10000000a4450e5.html

Option 😧 Substitutions are real and must be cleaned = Manage My Substitutes in My Inbox

Public Cloud documents substitution maintenance via My Inbox-> Manage My Substitutes

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/2c950e3d77504dd2a5dde48cdd4d43c4.html

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3521601

Option E: Your (E)WM PI tolerance example is correct some apps don’t offer search help by user

For Assign Tolerance Group Count Confirmation, SAP confirms no input help for User Name by design can be 3rd-party user so you must enter it explicitly

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3434972

Summary:

If your goal is show me every place this user is assigned the closest standard approach is a leaver checklist across:

  • Maintain Business Users (lock/validity + remove roles)
  • IAM Information System (confirm what access they still have / what changed)
  • Manage Teams and Responsibilities (replace/remove user in teams used by workflows)
  • My Inbox->Manage My Substitutes (remove substitution rules)

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Positioning SAP Sustainability Solutions: A Guide for Businesses
entry.content:
Sustainability is no longer just a buzzword—it’s a business imperative. Companies worldwide are under increasing pressure to reduce their environmental impact, comply with regulations, and meet stakeholder expectations. We offer a suite of SAP Sustainability Solutions designed to help organizations measure, manage, and optimize their sustainability performance.

Why SAP Sustainability Solutions Matter

SAP’s sustainability solutions provide businesses with the tools they need to:

  • Track and report emissions 
  • Optimize resource usage (energy, water, waste)
  • Ensure compliance with global regulations (e.g., CSRD, SEC climate rules)
  • Drive circular economy initiatives through better supply chain visibility

By integrating sustainability data with core business processes, companies can make informed decisions that align profitability with environmental responsibility.

Key Features of SAP Sustainability Solutions

  1. SAP Sustainability Footprint Management – Helps organizations calculate and reduce their carbon footprint.
  2. SAP Responsible Design and Production – Supports sustainable product lifecycle management.
  3. SAP Sustainability Data Exchange – Enables seamless data sharing across supply chains.
  4. SAP ESG Reporting – Simplifies compliance with evolving environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards.

Who Should Take the Course?

The Positioning SAP Sustainability Solutions course is ideal for:

  • Sustainability professionals looking to leverage SAP tools
  • Business leaders aiming to integrate sustainability into their strategy
  • IT and ERP consultants supporting sustainability initiatives

Conclusion

As sustainability becomes a key driver of business success, SAP’s solutions provide a structured way to embed ESG principles into operations. By taking this course, professionals can gain the knowledge needed to position SAP Sustainability Solutions effectively and help their organizations achieve long-term sustainability goals.

Ready to learn more? Explore the course here.

entry.title: Re: Process order that handles multiple sales orders
entry.content:
Hello @giopo49 ,

If you have 50 units for SO and 50 units for Warehouse, then try below approach.

Try this solution:

1. Set up the Strategy group = 40 in MRP View 3

2. Make Individual/ Collective Requirements = 2 in MRP View 4

3. For the Sales order demand create the SO.

4. For the warehouse demand, create a Planned Independent Requirement (PIR) via transaction MD61 for the "extra" 50 units you want for the warehouse.

5. MRP will see a total demand of 100 units. You can then combine these into one Process Order in the Stock/Requirements List (MD04)

6. When you post the Goods Receipt (MIGO), all 100 units go into Unrestricted Stock. You can ship 50 units to the customer and keep 50 units in the warehouse.

Vaibhav

entry.title: Re: Unable to pass filter parameter from the URL of CAPM...
entry.content:
What you are observing is expected behavior for a standalone SAP Fiori Elements List Report consuming an OData V4 CAP service.

In Fiori Elements, the Smart Filter Bar (SFB) is not automatically bound to arbitrary URL query parameters (?status=02) nor to raw OData $filter expressions passed via the launch URL. Instead, filters are applied through semantic navigation and startup parameters, which are resolved during app initialization.

Why the filter is not applied automatically

  1. URL query parameters are not interpreted as filters by default
    Parameters like ?status=02 are treated as generic query params and are ignored by the Filter Bar.

  2. Direct $filter in the URL is not consumed by the SFB
    While $filter=status eq '02' is a valid OData query, the List Report framework deliberately does not bind $filter from the URL to the Smart Filter Bar to ensure consistency, validation, and user awareness.

  3. @ui.SelectionFields only define filterable fields
    They do not imply automatic pre-filling or execution from URL parameters.

This behavior is by design and consistent with SAP Fiori Elements guidelines.

entry.title: Re: Unable to pass filter parameter from the URL of CAPM...
entry.content:
SAP-recommended approach (especially for mashups) Since your requirement is to embed the app as a mashup in Sales & Service Cloud v2 and contextually filter by Account, the correct and future-proof approach is: 1. Use semantic navigation with startup parameters Define a semantic object and action. Pass the Account ID / status as a startup parameter. Map it to the corresponding filter field via inbound navigation configuration. This ensures: The filter field is pre-filled The List Report executes automatically Behavior remains consistent across upgrades 2. Handle parameters via manifest.json Use inbound parameters and default values instead of relying on raw URL filters. This aligns with SAP Clean Core and upgrade-safe extensibility. 3. (Optional) Controller extension only if unavoidable If the launch context cannot be controlled (some mashup scenarios), a controller extension can read startup parameters and programmatically apply filters — but this should be treated as a last resort, not the default design. CAP-specific clarification There is no conflict between CAP CDS projections and filtering behavior here. The issue is purely UI-framework related, not a limitation of CAP or RAP. Your service is behaving correctly since filtering works once triggered via the UI.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: how to give new line in segment E1BPSDTEXT of idoc SA...
entry.content:
Create a test text in SO10 under Object = VBBK, ID = ZEIN.
Enter three lines manually:

  1. ABC1
  2. ABC2
  3. ABC3

Save and check in VA03. If this displays correctly, then the issue is with the IDoc processing logic, not the text configuration.
